issues.message: This file documents a fabricated group-controlled API for `Checkbox.Group` (`value`/`defaultValue`/`onValueChange`, implicit checked calculation from `HiddenInput.value`) that upstream does not expose. In `@seed-design/react`, `CheckboxGroupProps` is only `HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`, so these types and examples will not type-check or work.

This file treats `FieldButton.Root` as a standalone clickable input and marks `FieldButton.Button`/`FieldButton.Control` as unnecessary, but upstream `FieldButton.Root` is only a `<div>`. The interactive element lives in `FieldButton.Button`, and the root state API is `values`/`onValuesChange` rather than the documented `value`/`defaultValue`/`onValueChange`.

`onCheckedChange` is documented with the wrong signature. Upstream `useSwitch` defines it as `(checked: boolean) => void`, so the `{ checked }` object shape here and the later destructuring examples are invalid against the real API.

This changes `components[*].status` values in `--json` output from the legacy strings to new canonical tokens (`local-only`, `internal-primitive`, etc.). Adding `kind`/`note` is fine, but mutating `status` breaks backward compatibility for existing consumers that match the previous `status` strings.

The loading section claims `loading` auto-renders a `progressCircle` spinner/pseudo-element, but upstream `ToggleButton.tsx` only adds `data-loading`/`data-disabled` and `toggle-button.css` only changes background colors. There is no spinner DOM or CSS-generated progress circle to match this documentation.
This example imports icons from `@daangn/react-monochrome-icon`, but the upstream icon package used elsewhere in this snapshot is `@karrotmarket/react-monochrome-icon`.
